title: CV
text:

Royal Geographical Society (with IBG) Accredited
Modules include specialisation in Big Data, Urban Modelling and Simulation pathways:
Urban Systems Theory; Geographic Information Systems and Science; Quantitative Methods; Foundations of Spatial Data Science; Data Science for Spatial Systems; Agent-based Modelling for Spatial Systems; Building Spatial Applications with Big Data; Remotely Sensing Cities and Environments; Dissertation.





RICS Accredited, RTPI Spatially Accredited
Final Grade: Second Class with Honours, Division One (2.1)
Dissertation: Explore The Role of Vertical Greenery Systems in Enhancing Urban Energy Efficiency in Dense Urban Areas, Strategies for Hong Kong.
Modules include: Developed a strong understanding in urban theories, policies and application of planning law principles to real world case studies. Gained practical experiences in valuation and appraisal analyses, plan-making, and applied development projects incorporating spatial analyses, developed data analytics skills to support evidence-based decision-making approaches in urban design and research.
Coursework Projects: Wilmslow Baseline Analysis and Proposal Report; Trafford Local Plan-Making - Transport, Development Management; Planning Application for Residential Development at Thornham Old Road, Oldham – Site Potential Evaluation and Design and Access Statement; Site Analysis and Local Character and Facilities with Map Making Illustrations; M3 1FB Context Analyses and Sustainable Development Final Project; M1 4HD Office Valuation Report, Housing Market Insights Report of Greater Manchester; Woodford Aerodrome, Stockport Public Local Planning Enquiry

Global Summer School | 2022 | TSINGHUA UNIVERSITY, CHINA

Subject Theme: A Healthy Planet for Sustainable Development
Modules include: Understanding challenges and propose solutions utilising AI in achieving digital inclusion for sign language users, implementing AI to promote inclusivity for all.

A-Levels: Fine Art, Mathematics, Economics, Chinese (Traditional Chinese, Mandarin)
EPQ: Extended Project (Level 3) Qualifications 2022
Young Enterprise West England 2021- Best Innovation Award, Best Presentation Award, Best Report Award, Best Overall Award
GCSE: English Language, English Literature, Mathematics, Double Award in Science, Fine Art, Geography, German Language, Religious Studies

Assisted in material selection, planning and execution of landscape projects, on site showroom preparation for Central Peak property development.
Conducted a design project for a public park redevelopment (internship department assessment), translation of design concepts into practical solutions, using Auto CAD, Sketchup and Photoshop.



Design Assistant Internship | GYSO PEARLS & JEWELLERY LTD, Hong Kong | July 2020 – August 2020

title: About me…
text: I am currently a Master’s student at the Bartlett Centre for Advanced Spatial Analysis. I hold a Bachelor’s degree in Planning and Real Estate from the University of Manchester. I am experienced in cleaning and analysing complex datasets, producing clear reporting, and translating evidence into practical recommendations. I am committed to applying digital analytics to navigate complexity and support well-informed, inclusive, long-term solutions through evidence-based decision-making.

title: Coursework
text:
Welcome! This page showcases a collection of my academic coursework from my Master’s Degree in Urban Spatial Science and my Bachelor’s Degree in Planning and Real Estate. Both completed works and projects currently in progress are included in the below.
Note* this set of slides is for presentation purpose only, View the full-screen version at: Coursework Slides

title: Coursework
section: MSc Urban Spatial Science, University College London
text:
MSc Urban Spatial Science, University College London
Spatial Analysis - CASA0005 Geographic Information Systems and Science
TfL Vision Zero
This is a spatial hotspot analysis examining where deaths and serious injuries cluster across London in 2024 using casualty data provided by TfL. The analysis applied point pattern analysis methods and spatial autocorrelation methods to identify statistically significant hotspot and coldspot areas and discuss policy implications for Vision Zero.
View the full report (HTML): TfL Vision Zero
New York Evictions
This is a spatial hotspot analysis examining where eviction activities cluster across New York in 2020 using evictions data provided by NYC Open Data. The analysis applied point pattern analysis methods and spatial autocorrelation methods to identify statistically significant hotspot and coldspot areas, discussing areas to be prioritiesed for policy interventions.
View the full report (HTML): New York Evictions
Graffiti San Francisco
View the full report (HTML): coming soon…
CASA0007 Quantitative Methods
House Prices and London Underground: Does Multiple Interchange Tube Stations Link with Higher London House Prices in 2024?
Do multi-line Tube interchanges relate to higher house prices in London? This report analysed 82k+ property sales in London in 2024 from HM Land Registry Price Paid Data to test whether the connectivity level of the nearest Underground station (single-line vs 2-line vs 3+ line interchange) is associated with sale prices, controlling for property types, distance to central London, distance to nearest Tube station, and deprivation indicies (IMD). The final model explained a substantial variance (R² ≈ 0.527) and found higher prices near multi-line interchanges. Some local neighbourhood effects remain visible in the unexplained part of the model, supported by a positive Moran’s I (≈0.389). A technical appendix is included within the report.
View the full report (PDF): [London House Prices and Underground Regression Analysis 2024] coming soon…
CASA0023 Remotely Sensing Cities and Environments
View my Learning Diary (Webpage): CASA0023 Remotely Sensing Cities and Environments 25/26 Learning Diary
Note: This webpage is still in its building process, the full learning diary will be completed by April 2026.

title: Coursework
section: BSc Planning and Real Estate, University of Manchester
text:
BSc Planning and Real Estate, University of Manchester
Spatial Analysis, Transport - PLAN0010 Applied Project 22/23
This report analyses the transport infrastructure of Wilmslow, Cheshire, examining key themes including car ownership, public transport provision, and commuting patterns using data from the UK Census 2011 and Cheshire East Council. Spatial data analysis and choropleth mapping were produced using ArcGIS Pro, with supporting charts and data visualisations from Excel. A proposal report was also included, outlining actionable solutions of improved cycling infrastructure, car clubs, and travel card schemes to address the Wilmslow’s heavy car dependency and congestion issues.
View the full report (PDF): Wilmslow Baseline Analysis and Proposal Report
